name: Discourse
version: Current monthly channel / exact point release verify at implementation
version_status: CONFIRMED channel / point release not pinned
what: Mature community forum platform for discussions, knowledge threads, moderation, trust systems, and email-driven participation.
solves: Community support, customer discussion, announcements, and long-lived conversation spaces.
users: Communities, support teams, developer ecosystems, and membership organizations.
fit: Good fit if Hylono wants a real community, practitioner discussion, or peer support forum. It is broader and more community-capable than Apache Answer.
runtime: Rails/Ember application stack with PostgreSQL, Redis, Sidekiq jobs, and mail integration.
components: Web app, PostgreSQL, Redis, Sidekiq, uploads/storage, mail ingress/egress, and plugin ecosystem.
deploy: Official self-hosting is Docker-on-Linux oriented; production requires mail, storage, backups, and moderation operations.
deps: PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker/Linux host, mail delivery, storage, CDN/reverse proxy if scaled.
datastores: PostgreSQL and Redis plus uploads storage.
auth: Local accounts, SSO/OIDC options, moderation roles, and trust levels.
apis: REST API, webhooks, plugins, SSO, email ingestion, and admin surfaces.
security: Public communities need strong moderation, email security, plugin discipline, and abuse/spam controls.
setup_path: Use official Docker install path only if Hylono genuinely wants a forum/community surface.
env: Hostnames, SMTP, storage/uploads, Redis/PostgreSQL, SSO config, and admin/moderation settings.
persistence: Back up PostgreSQL, Redis (as needed), and uploads; preserve moderation and content retention policies.
operations: Moderation, spam prevention, upgrade cadence, plugin compatibility, and email deliverability are ongoing responsibilities.
failure_points: Launching a forum without community operations capacity, or using a full forum where a lighter Q&A/knowledge base would suffice.
integration: Can integrate with ZITADEL/OIDC, site identity links, Novu/Listmonk announcements, and knowledge workflows.
verify: Confirm whether Hylono wants ongoing community discussion or just structured Q&A/help.
risk: Moderate product/ops risk; community tooling needs human stewardship.
gdpr: User accounts and posts are personal data. Retention/moderation/export processes matter.
recommendation: CHOOSE ONLY IF COMMUNITY IS STRATEGIC
overlap: Choose Discourse over Apache Answer if broader community/forum capability matters.
codex_read_first: Read the overlap map with Apache Answer, BookStack, and Docusaurus first.
codex_best_path: Use Discourse only if Hylono commits to community moderation and discussion as a strategic surface.
codex_never_assume: Never assume a forum will self-sustain without moderation and editorial effort.

sources.why: Primary code source and release channel anchor.
